items.title: Class action claims women were raped and stalked at remote Fortescue mines

children.content: Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest's mining giant Fortescue has been slapped with a landmark class action alleging systemic sexual harassment, discrimination and toxic work sites.

items.title: Australia flags strengthening of social media ban

children.content: Australia will strengthen its social media ban for young people as a priority amid concerns the powers behind the world-first laws are not strong enough.

image.altText: A man standing at a microphone in federal parliament.

items.title: Hanson faces scrutiny as media landscape shifts around her

children.content: Parliament House has been Pauline's World lately but increased visibility comes with increased scrutiny and this week revealed that One Nation is not untouchable.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson fire the liar APH

images.altText: A car is smashed by the debris of a collapsed building
A small group of people stand and talk in front of a white building with large cracks in its exterior at night.

children.content: Two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, local time, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas.

image.altText: rubble and a collapsed building visited by emergency services

children.content: Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodríguez says at least 971 others have been confirmed injured after emergency services worked through the night to find those trapped under collapsed buildings. Follow live.

items.title: At least 164 people killed after back-to-back earthquakes rock Venezuela

updates.title: UN-coordinated specialist rescue teams on the way
Countries around the world sending rescuers, resources to Venezuela
At least 164 people dead, acting president says

items.title: How Karl Stefanovic went from Today show glory to 'Joe Bogan'

children.content: Today show host Karl Stefanovic is reportedly leaving Channel Nine after a more than 20-year career at the network that was filled with highs and lows.

image.altText: Mr Stefanovic wearing a black blazer and bow tie with a white dress shirt standing and smiling.

items.title: Former Liberal leader Matthew Guy denies assaulting MP Moira Deeming

children.content: Victorian MP Matthew Guy is considering legal advice to sue his party room colleague for defamation over the assault allegation, which is being investigated by police.

image.altText: Moira Deeming and Matthew Guy at an event

items.title: Rabbitohs overrun plucky Eels to spoil milestone celebration for Moses

children.content: South Sydney have defeated the Parramatta Eels to record back-to-back wins in the NRL for the first time since April. Follow live.

image.altText: Edward Kosi of the Rabbitohs scores a try with two defenders on him

items.title: Defending champs Lions finding top gear against Swans at Gabba

children.content: The defending champions, Brisbane, are threatening to score a big win over second-placed Sydney at the Gabba. Follow live.

image.altText: A Brisbane Lions AFL player grins and

items.title: Shark attack victim 'remembers the whole event in detail'

children.content: The 34-year-old's brother said she was awake, speaking with loved ones and her medical team and "remembers the whole event in detail".

gallery.altText: Sydney mother and Coogee local, Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark holds a baby and looks at the camera

thumbnail.altText: Sydney mother and Coogee local, Leah Stewart, who was mauled by a shark holds a baby and looks at the camera

items.title: Parent asks if 'warning signs' missed after Chinese language school closure

children.content: The shock closure of a Chinese language school after 17 years leaves parents and teachers with "unanswered questions" over the school's financial management.

image.altText: A group of teachers with faces blurred.

items.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route amid high cost, falling demand

children.content: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

image.altText: A photo of a Qantas plane flying in the sky.

items.title: Laptop at the pub or office watch party: How Australian workers plan to watch the Socceroos

children.content: The countdown is on for Australia's World Cup match against Paraguay, and some football fans are preparing to happily put work on the backburner.

articles.title: The calls for Australia to play its best attacking weapons are loud

children.content: Tony Popovic dances to his own selection tune, but that doesn't mean there aren't loud calls for him to select Australia's most attacking weapons against Paraguay.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato #20 of Australia strikes the ball during the FIFA World Cup

articles.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill (left) and Harry Souttar look on

articles.title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game

children.content: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

image.altText: Jacob Italiano #4 of Australia controls the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026

items.title: 'Some cracks' appearing in jobs market, even as unemployment rate dips

children.content: Australia's unemployment rate slipped to 4.4 per cent in May, down from 4.5 per cent in April, in seasonally adjusted terms.

image.altText: Boxing Day sales Pitt Street mall Sydney

items.title: 'Everybody is equal at the table': Inside the ACT's Circle Sentencing court

children.content: A pilot program allowing First Nations offenders to be sentenced by magistrates sitting alongside elders in the ACT Supreme Court secures funding for another three years.

image.altText: A person in a dark shirt stands inside an office.

items.title: Ghost nets transformed into art to highlight pollution's 'devastating' impact

children.content: Thousands of tonnes of ocean waste lands on Australia's shores every year. These Queenslanders have found a creative use for some of it.

items.title: Taking the plunge in chilly winter water to tackle loneliness

children.content: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

items.title: When can gambling become an addiction? Here are the warning signs

children.content: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

items.title: Australians' tricks for dealing with heatwaves that Europeans yet to learn

children.content: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

gallery.altText: Four people playing in water near the Eiffel Tower

items.title: Ex-AFL umpire to stand trial over alleged Brownlow fraud

children.content: Ex-AFL umpire Michael Pell, accused of leaking information on confidential Brownlow votes, has been ordered to stand trial.

image.altText: An image of Michael Pell.

items.title: Big battery boom means fewer transmission lines will be needed

children.content: Home batteries hit 600,000 as households play big role in energy transition, but building transmission projects remains "critical", according to the latest AEMO energy plan.

image.altText: Michael McGarvie carbon-gel battery system at his home in Eaglemont, on October 20, 2015.

items.title: Sea bird sick with H5 avian flu landed by bodyboarders, too weak to fly to shore

children.content: A surfer who spotted the first bird in South Australia to test positive for H5N1 bird flu says the giant petrel looked fatigued and distressed before it was retrieved by wildlife rescuers.

image.altText: A man with white hair and a black hoodie smiles at the camera

items.title: Antic's 'dangerous' comments on vaccinations condemned

children.content: South Australian Liberal senator Alex Antic has been roundly condemned over comments he made about vaccinations after avian influenza was found on Australia's southern coast.

image.altText: A close up of Alex Antic speaking in the Senate chamber, in a suit with a red tie

items.title: Traffic cones on statues and a sea of orange: The traditions taking over the World Cup

children.content: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

articles.title: What today's results mean for each World Cup team in action

children.content: The path to the World Cup knockouts is more complicated than ever. Here is what Thursday's results mean for the path ahead.

items.title: Teals Allegra Spender and Zali Steggall form new political party

children.content: The teal independents have confirmed they are forming a new political party, Community Strong Australia, but are yet to convince crossbench colleagues to join what they call a "responsible alternative" to the major parties.

image.altText: Two women taking on radio into microphones.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' barred from Australia issued permit to return

children.content: Australia's home affairs minister approves the return of a woman who has links to Islamic State and has been in a Syrian refugee camp, saying the government plans to have her under high-level surveillance.

image.altText: A woman in a black niqab walks along a row of tents, with children seen in the distance.

items.title: Conspiracy theorist podcaster jailed for stabbing girlfriend to death with dagger

children.content: Tobias Nuttall is sentenced to at least 18 years behind bars for murdering his girlfriend with a 25-centimetre dagger in a "vicious" attack in her suburban Perth home.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man with long brown hair and a moustache, talking while appearing on a video call for a podcast.

items.title: ABC fishing star rescued from rough seas after boat capsizes

children.content: The voice of John "Stinker" Clarke is well known to those who love to wet a line, but there was no-one around to hear a cry for help when his boat capsized north of Newcastle.

image.altText: A beared man in a hat leans on a boat

items.title: As Ebola spreads, Australia faces rise in 'biblical' disease risks

children.content: The bugs that cause Ebola, hantavirus and diphtheria are microscopic, but many of the barriers to controlling them are human-sized.

items.title: Olympian arrested at reflecting pool is Trump 'scapegoat', lawyer says

children.content: A lawyer for the Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool says his client is a scapegoat who was targeted to distract Americans from the Trump administration's failures.

image.altText: People gather near the reflecting pool, including a person waving a large American flag.

items.title: Tribute to 'respected' WA pilot after fatal flight in plane he built himself

children.content: A friend of 76-year-old pilot Malcolm Vivian remembers him as a "beautiful man" and "special aircraft builder" after his death in a light plane crash near Northam, and says he does not think pilot error was to blame.

image.altText: Two men piloting a light aircraft wearing headsets

items.title: Understanding of harmful algal blooms still murky, disaster survey finds

children.content: Most Australians remain uncertain about the cause of harmful algal blooms and are more worried about disasters such as bushfires and flooding.

items.title: Triple fatality prompts medical certificate call for learner drivers

children.content: South Australia's deputy coroner recommends learner drivers obtain a medical certificate affirming their ability to drive before they get a licence.

image.altText: A woman leans against an older man in a hat and striped shirt and an older woman wearing glasses, all smiling.

items.title: Woman fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars

children.content: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

image.altText: A woman with black haair close-up looking sad

items.title: Small designers hitting roadblocks trying to get support against Shein copies

children.content: Small Australian fashion designers who have had their designs copied by overseas ultra-fast fashion companies say they have hit continuous roadblocks trying to get support.
